summary: Serious incident reporting workflow (Article 73) used by providers to triage, escalate, and notify competent authorities about events that meet the regulatory definition of a serious incident. GovAI exports can support the evidence package; the legal notification is operator-owned.

trigger_criteria.description: Incident resulted in or could have resulted in serious physical or psychological harm to a person.
Incident caused serious disruption to critical infrastructure as defined by EU or member state law.
Incident caused infringement of obligations under Union law intended to protect fundamental rights.
Incident caused serious damage to property or the environment attributable to the AI system.

workflow_steps.title: Detection and intake
actions: Capture incident from monitoring, support, or external report.
Record initial timestamp, system version, and reporter.

workflow_steps.title: Severity triage
actions: Confirm or rule out serious incident classification.
Engage regulatory owner if classification is uncertain.

workflow_steps.title: Containment and mitigation
actions: Apply operational mitigations (pause, rollback, guardrail change).

workflow_steps.title: Authority notification
actions: Attach evidence including GovAI exports where helpful.
Send notification through the channel required by the competent authority.

workflow_steps.title: Reporting and closure
actions: Document root cause and corrective actions.
Update risk register and post-market monitoring plan.

boundaries: Deadlines are indicative defaults; the binding timelines are set by Article 73 and implementing acts.
GovAI does not transmit notifications to authorities; operators handle external communications.
Confidentiality and personal data protections still apply to attached evidence.
